ocr.text: The table presents various measures related to self-management behavior, healthcare utilization, and clinical outcomes,
detailing overall frequencies and corresponding weighted adjusted odds ratios (OR) or hazard ratios (HR) with 95% confidence
intervals (CI). For self-management behavior, capillary blood glucose monitoring shows an overall frequency of 63.1% with a
weighted adjusted OR of 0.05 (0.02–0.08). In healthcare utilization, the overall frequency of ophthalmologic exams is 74.3%
with a weighted adjusted OR of 0.82 (0.56–1.22), while specialist visits have an overall frequency of 41.9% and a weighted
adjusted OR of 0.55 (0.37–0.86).
Measure
Self-management behavior  Overall frequency  Weighted adjusted OR (95% CI)*
Capillary blood glucose monitoring †  63.1%  0.05 (0.02–0.08)
Health care utilization  Overall frequency  Weighted adjusted OR (95% CI) *
Ophthalmologic exam  74.3%  0.82 (0.56–1.22)
Specialist visit  41.9%  0.55 (0.37–0.86)
Clinical outcomes  Overall event occurrence  Weighted adjusted HR (95% CI) *
Hypo- or hyperglycemia  4.8%  0.09 (0.01–0.28)
Soft tissue infection or gangrene  3.1%  0.47 (0.11–1.28)
Myocardial infarction  5.5%  0.92 (0.32–1.50)
Regarding clinical outcomes, hypo- or hyperglycemia occurs at a frequency of 4.8% with a weighted adjusted HR of 0.09
(0.01–0.28), soft tissue infection or gangrene is reported at 3.1% with a weighted adjusted HR of 0.47 (0.11–1.28), myocardial
infarction has a frequency of 5.5% with a weighted adjusted HR of 0.92 (0.32–1.50), and death occurs at a frequency of 13.9%
with a weighted adjusted HR of 1.02 (0.67–1.59). Each measure provides critical insights into patient behaviors and outcomes,
emphasizing the importance of monitoring and healthcare engagement in managing health conditions effectively. The data
highlights significant differences in behavior and outcomes, underscoring the role of healthcare interventions in influencing
these metrics.
